title: bomarea goniocaulon subsp. elegans
intro: Bomarea goniocaulon subsp. elegans, also known as the 'Elegant Bomarea', is a species of flowering plant in the family Alstroemeriaceae, characterized by its showy, white and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties contribute to several health benefits, including relieving pain and reducing inflammation in the body, as well as supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the Elegant Bomarea is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be applied topically or consumed orally for medicinal purposes.
